question_title: How many new entries will be added to CISA’s KEV catalog between February 17, 2026 and April 30, 2026?
summary: Forecasting CISA KEV catalog additions between Feb 17 - Apr 30, 2026. 18 entries are already locked in (Feb 17 - Mar 5), with 56 days remaining. The 2026 YTD pace (0.81/day) is ~21% above 2025's rate (0.67/day), continuing an upward trend. Historical comparable windows show high variance: 2023=39, 2024=22, 2025=56. CISA has lost ~30% of its workforce to budget cuts, which could modestly slow additions but is unlikely to halt the core KEV function. A Monte Carlo mixture model (3 scenarios: budget-cuts slowdown 20% weight, base case 55%, acceleration 25%) produces a median of ~60 with P10=46 and P90=76.

factors.description: 18 entries already in window (Feb 17 - Mar 5), establishing a floor and 1.06/day pace in the window so far
2026 YTD rate of 0.81/day is higher than 2025 full-year rate of 0.67/day, suggesting continued acceleration in vulnerability exploitation
Historical comparable windows: 2023=39, 2024=22, 2025=56 - wide variance but clear upward trend
CISA budget cuts: staffing reduced from 3400 to 2400 (30%), could slow KEV catalog operations
factors.conditional: If cuts directly impact KEV team operations

factors.description: KEV additions driven primarily by threat landscape; core function likely preserved even with cuts
Monte Carlo mixture model (3 scenarios weighted by likelihood) produces median ~60 with P10=46, P90=76

reasoning:
**Forecast: CISA KEV Catalog Additions (Feb 17 - Apr 30, 2026)**

| Percentile | Value |
|------------|-------|
| P10 | 46 |
| P20 | 50 |
| P40 | 57 |
| P60 | 63 |
| P80 | 71 |
| P90 | 76 |

**Key findings:**
- **18 entries already counted** (Feb 17 - Mar 5), with 56 days remaining in the window
- **2026 pace is accelerating** at 0.81/day YTD vs 2025's 0.67/day annual rate
- **Historical comparable windows** show high variance (22-56 in 2024-2025), but the trend is upward
- **CISA budget cuts** (workforce down 30%) create downside risk, but KEV catalog is a core function likely to be preserved
- **Median estimate: ~60 entries**, with an 80% confidence interval of roughly 50-71
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the integer count of entries in CISA’s Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog whose dateAdded is between 2026-02-17 and 2026-04-30 (inclusive of both dates), as determined from the official CISA KEV catalog data when checked by Metaculus on or after May 1, 2026. The primary source is CISA’s KEV catalog page at [https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog](https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog)
fine_print: Count entries by the catalog’s dateAdded field, not by publish time or dueDate. If CISA corrects entries (including dateAdded) before May 1, 2026, use the latest catalog state available before May 1, 2026. If the data download/feed linked from the primary page is inaccessible, count using the catalog’s official table export (CSV/JSON) if available; if neither is accessible, resolve using credible sources per [https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) while prioritizing CISA-hosted data. Candidate Sources: CISA KEV JSON feed (if accessible) [https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known\_exploited\_vulnerabilities.json](https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json)
background_info:
The pace of KEV additions is a proxy for the flow of actively exploited vulnerabilities that require urgent remediation.
